title: Lab 08: Autofs With NFS
description: Configure an indirect automount from server

tasks: On server, export /exports/vault8. On client, create user vault8 with password cinder9.
Configure autofs on client so /netdir/vault8 is mounted on demand from server:/exports/vault8 and persists across reboot.
hints: The export already contains a file named welcome.txt.
checks: showmount -e server | grep -Eq '(^|[[:space:]])/exports/vault8([[:space:]]|$)' && getent passwd vault8 >/dev/null && getent shadow vault8 | awk -F: 'length($2)>0 && $2 !~ /^(!!?|\*|LK|NP)$/' >/dev/null

mount | grep -Eq 'server:/exports/vault8 on /netdir/vault8 type nfs' && test -f /netdir/vault8/welcome.txt && grep -Fqx 'autofs lab 08' /netdir/vault8/welcome.txt && grep -Fqx '/netdir /etc/auto.lab8' /etc/auto.master.d/lab8.autofs
solution_commands: for attempt in 1 2 3 4 5; do ls /netdir/vault8 >/dev/null 2>&1 && mount | grep -Eq 'server:/exports/vault8 on /netdir/vault8 type nfs' && break; sleep 2; done
